rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN T. WEST et al VS. LAWRENCE EBRINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Lawrence Ebringer'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admission, Set One, Judicial Council Interrogatories, Set 2 And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 1, 2014, Line 2, PART 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING - Defendant Lawrence Ebringer'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admission, Set One, Judicial Council Form Interrogatories, Set 2 And Request For Production Of Documents FI 9.1: The objection that the term "incident," is vague and ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs' attorney client privilege and attorney work product objections are SUSTAINED. Plaintiffs shall provide complete and straightforward responses to subparts (a) - (d). FI 17.1: The objection that the term "prepared," is vague and ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs shall provide complete and straightforward responses to subparts (a) - (d) as to RFAs 1-2, 4-9, 14, and 16. RFP 15: Plaintiffs' attorney-client privilege and attorney work product objections are SUSTAINED. Plaintiffs' objection as to "any other applicable privilege¿" is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs' objection that the "interrogatory (sic) seeks expert witness information is SUSTAINED. Plaintiffs objection that the request "seeks information of third parties¿" is OVERRULED. The objection that the terms "estimate," "work," and "must be performed" are ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs' relevancy objection is OVERRULED. Sanctions in the amount of $1,000 are awarded against Plaintiffs Stephen West and Aimee West. Verified, amended responses to be provided by Plaintiff within 10 days of service of this order. Plaintiffs' counsel is reminded to comply with CRC Rule 3.1113(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN T. WEST et al VS. LAWRENCE EBRINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admission, Set One, Judicial Council Form Interrogatories, Set Two, And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 1, 2014, Line 2, PART 1 OF TENTATIVE RULING - CROSS COMPLAINANT LAWRENCE EBRINGER's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admission, Set One, Judicial Council Form Interrogatories, Set Two, And Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED as follows: RFAs 1-2, 4-9, 14, and 16: The objection that the "request seeks information equally available to the propounding party" is OVERRULED. The objection that the term "prepared," is vague and ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs shall provide verified, amended responses to these RFAs according to the definition clarified in the March 20, 2014 letter from Plaintiffs' counsel. RFAs 24-40: The objection that the term "reviewed," is vague and ambiguous and unintelligible is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs shall provide verified, amended responses to these RFAs according to the definition clarified in the March 20, 2014 letter from Plaintiffs' counsel. RFAs 3, 10-13,15, 17-23: The objection that the term "received," is vague and ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs shall provide verified, amended responses to these RFAs according to the definition clarified in the March 20, 2014 letter from Plaintiffs' counsel.

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Qush Service Of Subpoena Duces Tecum; Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 1, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT HAROLD WONG's motion To Quash Service Of Subpoena Duces Tecum; Req. For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion to Quash is DENIED. The request for sanctions is denied. Defendant's Objection to Plaintiff's unauthenticated evidence is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part, Defendant admits that the Wells Fargo documents were produced by Defendant himself (Reply 4:6-8). Therefore the objection to Exhibit 2 is OVERRULED. The objections to Exhibits 1, 4-5 are SUSTAINED. Exhibit 3 needs no authentication since it is an order of this Court. Defendant's relevancy objection is OVERRULED. The checks to American Express in the Wells Fargo documents produced by Defendant reference the American Express account numbers that appear in the subpoena to American Express. Defendant's objection that the subpoena is over broad is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part, Defendant admits that he managed the LLC from 2005 to October 2012 (Mtn., 2:11-12). The subpoena requests documents from January 2006 to January 2013. The Subpoena is limited in time to January 2006 through October 2012. Defendant's privacy objection is OVERRULED. Defendant himself produced the Wells Fargo records in discovery. It is therefore assumed that the Wells Fargo records contain relevant information. Plaintiff is entitled to know what charges were incurred on the American Express account. Defendant submitted no evidence to show that the American Express charges are for private purposes. Both parties are reminded to comply with CRC Rule 3.1110(f) when submitting courtesy papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS VS. JOHN CHIANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prelim Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 1, 2014, Line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANTS JOHN CHIANG, in his official capacity as CONTROLLER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, and THE OFFICE OF THE STATE CONTROLLER'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Continued to July 21, 2014 on the Court's motion. Judge Goldsmith requests that the parties file a short supplemental brief (no more than 7 pages) by July 14, 2014, addressing the effect, if any, Plaintiff's appeal of the denial of its motion for preliminary injunction has on this motion. Specifically, the parties should address whether the court retains jurisdiction to rule on this preliminary injunction pending appellate review and whether it would serve judicial economy to delay ruling on this motion until after the appeal is resolved. rulings.Case Title: ANA EDITH CONTRERAS et al VS. D'ANDRE WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Of Plaintiffs' Motion To Quash And/Or To Impose Conditions On White'S Deposition Subpoenas Seeking Consumer Records From Old Republic Title
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 1, 2014, Line 5, Hearing Of Plaintiffs' Motion To Quash And/Or To Impose Conditions On White'S Deposition Subpoenas Seeking Consumer Records From Old Republic Title Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance Required. The Motion to Compel and Motion to Quash pertain to the same April 18, 2014 Subpoena to Old Republic Title Company ("ORTC"). The Motion to Compel is GRANTED in part. The Motion to Quash is GRANTED in part. Counsel is to meet and confer before the hearing as to what specific "personal identifying information" and "private financial information" should be redacted by ORTC before the documents are produced. ORTC shall produce all redacted documents that are responsive to the subpoena within 10 days of service of this order. All requests for sanctions are DENIED. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: IDA JACK-VEASEY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 1, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF IDA JACK-VEASEY's motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. The Court shall conduct an in camera review of the medical records identified by the Plaintiff's counsel in the Reply at 4:10-13. The Court will conduct an analysis consistent with the Court's ruling in Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal. 3d 844 ((1) plaintiffs, while they may not withhold information that relates to any physical or mental condition that they put in issue by bringing the lawsuit, are entitled to retain the confidentiality of all unrelated past treatments (20 Cal. 3d 844, 864) and (2) defendant would be entitled to discover information relating to illness or injuries which, although not attributed to defendant's conduct, contributed to a medical condition put in issue by plaintiff (20 Cal. 3d 844, 864 n.9)). Plaintiff's counsel will provide a courtesy copy of the medical records at the hearing. Plaintiff's counsel is reminded to comply with CRC Rule 3.1110(f) when submitting courtesy papers. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MASTERS VS. WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISOR, LL,
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 1, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT BARBARA MCKEE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") does not state a cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing against Defendants Barbara Jean Schassar McKee and Andre Hakkak because it does not allege that these Defendants had any specific contractual obligations. (Racine & Laramie, Ltd. v. Dept. of Parks and Recreation (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 1026, 1031, "[t]he implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing rests upon the existence of some specific contractual obligation.") Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts establishing a contractual obligation, or to state a cause of action for intentional interference with contractual relations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
